iteration_details: Findings from Round 1 (architectural risks) informed Round 2 (operational gaps that compound those risks, e.g., no eviction policy worsens workload mixing; no TTLs worsen the flush-on-deploy strategy). Round 3 examined security/compliance implications surfaced by earlier rounds (e.g., session data in Redis requires security posture given its criticality established in Round 1).

round_1.description: Evaluated the core architectural decisions: Redis as primary store, single-instance topology, invalidation strategy, and workload co-location.

round_2.description: Examined missing operational concerns that compound the architectural risks: memory planning, TTLs, monitoring, and client configuration.

round_3.description: Assessed security posture for session data in Redis and rate-limiting resilience to adversarial manipulation.
